l1: Which specific user account or security principal is getting locked out?
When did the repeated lockout cycles start happening to this account?
Does the lockout clear temporarily upon administrator manual unlock actions?
l2: Is the lockout happening from a domain workstation, server, or external VPN?
Are there multiple devices or applications simultaneously submitting old credentials?
Is the lockout threshold configuration policy set domain-wide or via FGPP?

l1: Query the PDC Emulator for Event ID 4740 logs identifying the lockout origin.
Run 'net user <username> /domain' to verify current lock status and lock time.
Use the LockoutStatus tool to check state across all active domain controllers.
l2: Search Security logs for Event IDs 4625 to find bad password attempt data details.
Inspect the caller computer name field listed inside the 4740 event properties.
Check network connection sessions to map persistent open authentications.

l1: Unlock the user account utilizing Active Directory Administrative Center UI.
Have the user update passwords on known mobile devices and persistent apps.
Clear cached network credentials from the Windows Credential Manager vault.
l2: Identify and terminate old mapped network drives or scheduled background tasks.
Restart persistent Windows Services configured with outdated user service accounts.
Flush cached interactive web application credentials causing invisible background calls.

l1: Collect text outputs from LockoutStatus tool executed against the target domain.
Export Event 4740 logs from all Domain Controllers in the infrastructure.
Gather local client security authentication failure logs during reproduction.
l2: Enable Netlogon verbose debug logging via 'nltest /dbflag:0x2080ffff' on target controllers.
Collect network captures filtering on Kerberos and NTLM traffic from client source.
Dump active scheduled tasks lists from the identified caller workstation machine.

persistent_lockout_source.title: Locating and remediating persistent user account lockouts

steps.expect: Account status should output 'Account active: Lockout'
Extract Caller Computer Name to uncover the actual origin workstation
Open credential UI on source client and remove outdated entries for the domain
Account remains in unlocked status without triggering successive 4740 events
